sessions.title: Session 1 — Split Step Fundamentals
sessions.summary: Introductory session focused on building a strong split step foundation. Covered proper athletic posture, explosive push-off mechanics, and directional recovery patterns. No video recordings for this session.

drills.name: Ready Position & Resistance Band Work
drills.description: Half-squat athletic stance with resistance band lateral, forward, and backward movement patterns.
drills.notes: Stay on the balls of your feet — keep the band tension throughout

drills.name: Split Step Reaction — Drop & Catch
drills.description: Shuttle dropped from height; execute a split step and catch it before it lands.
drills.notes: Focus on timing the split step, not just catching the shuttle

drills.name: Bench Drop into Split Step
drills.description: Step off a low bench, land with a split step, then push off in a given direction.
drills.notes: The drop exaggerates the loading phase — feel the rhythm before adding speed

drills.name: Directional Recovery
drills.description: Shuttle thrown to mid-court; split step, read direction, recover, then move to the net.
drills.notes: Lead with the correct foot based on the direction you need to move

tips.text: The split step is not a jump — it's a quick, controlled drop that loads energy for an explosive push-off
Initiate your split step just before your opponent makes contact with the shuttle
Always return to the ready position with a split step after every shot
Land with one foot slightly ahead of the other to create a directional advantage

videos.title: The Split Step in Badminton — Badminton Insight

sessions.title: Session 2 — Clears & Rear Court Movement
sessions.takeaway: Both students demonstrated strong clear technique from a stationary position, showing noticeable improvement in timing and coordination following the tennis ball throwing drill and comparison with Coach Z's reference video. The primary area for continued development is rear court movement — transitioning from executing clears while standing still to performing them with proper footwork and court coverage.
sessions.summary: This session focused on overhead clear technique and rear court footwork. Students began by comparing their clear form against Coach Z's demonstration, then used tennis ball throwing drills to reinforce proper mechanics and shoulder activation. The improved timing carried over into shuttle feeding, where both students hit confident clears from a stationary position. The session concluded with split step footwork and rear court movement patterns.

drills.name: Clear Technique Comparison
drills.description: Side-by-side comparison of student clears against Coach Z's demonstration to identify form differences in contact point, body rotation, and follow-through.
drills.notes: Compare your form to Coach Z's video — pay attention to elbow height, weight transfer, and the snap at contact

drills.name: Tennis Ball Throw
drills.description: Overhead throwing motion with a tennis ball to reinforce throwing mechanics and shoulder activation. Builds the correct arm swing pattern used in clears and smashes.
drills.notes: Both students showing excellent timing and coordination — the throwing motion translates directly into a better clear

drills.name: Clear with Shuttle Feeding (Stationary)
drills.description: Coach-fed shuttles to practice clears from a fixed position — no movement, focusing purely on stroke technique. Emphasis on applying the timing and coordination developed from the tennis ball throw drill.
drills.notes: The improvement from the tennis ball throw is evident here — focus on consistency and keeping the shuttle deep

drills.name: Split Step & Footwork to the Rear Court
drills.description: Combining split step timing from Session 1 with rear court footwork patterns — reading the shuttle direction and moving explosively to the back of the court.
drills.notes: Split step first, then pivot and drive off the outside foot to move to the rear court

drills.name: Rear Court Movement & Shots
drills.description: Full rear court drill combining footwork with overhead shot execution. Moving to the shuttle, hitting the clear, and recovering to base position.
drills.notes: This is the area that needs the most work — use a scissor kick to recover and avoid falling backwards after the shot

tips.text: The throwing motion for a clear is the same as throwing a ball — if you can throw, you can clear
Hit the shuttle at the highest point you can reach — don't let it drop to your shoulder
A good clear should push your opponent deep behind the back service line
Always recover to base position after every rear court shot — don't admire your shot
